mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator is a Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources using Kubernetes custom resources. It is ideal for users who prefer to manage their observability stack through GitOps workflows or infrastructure as code. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRDs and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Grafana Operator Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using the specified version.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create Grafana Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Grafana instance using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> This command creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify Grafana Instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> This command retrieves the details of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss for Grafana instances and dashboards.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the Grafana instance and associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your Grafana configurations. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backup the current Grafana instance configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating Grafana instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD for Grafana is not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana CRD is installed by running 'kubectl get crd | grep grafana'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Grafana instance is still initializing or if there are configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana operator using 'kubectl logs deployment/grafana-operator'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the Grafana service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc grafana'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid admin password'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs if the admin password is not set correctly in the Grafana CR.

**Fix:**
Update the Grafana CR with the correct admin password and apply it again.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get grafana'.

    security:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
